title: Salesforce Field-Level Security Configuration Drift
dataSources: `index=salesforce` **`salesforce:event_log`**: Salesforce **ELF** via **ELF REST API** with default **hourly TA polling**; **Event Monitoring add-on / Shield** prerequisites apply. Narrow to **`EventType="SetupAuditTrail"`**, **`Section="Field-Level Security"`**, exposing actions like **`Change Field Accessibility`** with fields **`Display`** (friendly field label/path), **`DelegateUser`** (actor), **`Action`**, optional **`Entity`**, and timestamps—expand regex glossary using internal **data classification** metadata. **`RowsProcessed`** is generally irrelevant here; emphasis is textual drift on sensitive labels. Data flows collector→index unchanged. **CIM note:** **No CIM**—use stewardship spreadsheets to map **`Display`** entries to PHI/PCI tags for reporting.
app: `Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)), ≥5.0. Install on the Search Head (for field extractions and saved searches) and on a dedicated Heavy Forwarder if running data collection off-SH. The TA provides both collection and parsing. The TA requires a Salesforce Connected App with OAuth 2.0 credentials and an Event Monitoring or Salesforce Shield license.
spl:
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="SetupAuditTrail" Section="Field-Level Security"
| stats values(Action) as actions, count by Display, DelegateUser, _time
| where match(Display, "(?i)(ssn|social|credit|salary|birthdate|passport|account_number)")
| sort -_time
description: Tracks Setup Audit Trail changes under Field-Level Security that mention sensitive identifiers—such as tax, payroll, passport, payment, or account fields—surfacing widening visibility to profiles or permission sets tied to regulated data.
value: Silent widening of sensitive field access triggers privacy violations, CRA and PCI scrutiny, and failed audits; proactive drift alerts preserve least-privilege stories for regulators and keep customer trust intact when teams rush feature releases.
implementation: Maintain a glossary of canonical sensitive field labels; ingest SetupAuditTrail continuously; delegate review to data stewards who can reconcile Display values with Salesforce metadata dictionaries.
visualization: **Row 1 —** Interactive timeline of FLS events (**zoom default 7 days**, brush selects maintenance). **Row 2 —** Treemap **`Entity` → `Display`** sized by **`count`** of actions (shows concentrated objects). **Row 3 —** Analyst table (**`DelegateUser`, regex-classified severity, ticket link**). **Row 4 —** **Diff panel** contrasting current vs **`summaryindex`** snapshot for clicked field demonstrating before/after visibility states.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce — Data Collection Configuration

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Data-masking & tokenization uplift programs:** teams broaden FLS transiently to test partial visibility with synthetic data—**mitigate** `dmp_project` lookup with project code, data steward on-call, Slack pre-notify channel, and downgrade when ticket present. (b) **Post-merger harmonization sweeps:** thousands of fields change access in planned waves—**mitigate** `mna_freeze` macro with CIO sign-off, bifurcate alerts (prod vs integration org), sample-based human review instead of full paging. (c) **Sandbox/demo refreshes cloning prod labels:** benign noise mimics sensitive production names—**mitigate** route non-prod `OrganizationId` separately, require `sf_env=prod` macro in alert SPL. (d) **Managed packages & metadata push pipelines:** vendor namespaces rename similarly to internal secret fields—**mitigate** whitelist known namespaces, correlate CI/CD deployment IDs, escalate only on unscoped namespace matches. (e) **Pure localization / translation updates:** `Display` strings change while ACL matrix identical—**mitigate** maintain before/after snapshots in summary index, diff effective FLS hash weekly, suppress when only label hash changed. (f) **Hotfix nights & agile cutover:** rapid toggles spike SetupAuditTrail counts—**mitigate** map to `release_train` calendar + ServiceNow change bridging, require dual approval for suppression extensions beyond 24h.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **`Splunk Add-on for Salesforce` ≥5.0** on **Search Heads** (field extractions, saved searches, dashboards) and, for production hygiene, a **Heavy Forwarder** that performs OAuth-authenticated REST collection away from interactive admin sessions.
• **Salesforce entitlements & app security:** **Event Monitoring add-on** and/or **Salesforce Shield** for **Event Log File (ELF) REST** access; **Connected App** with **OAuth 2.0**; least-privilege integration user; credentials in Splunk **storage/passwords** or your secret manager—not cleartext in `local/inputs.conf`.
• **Indexes & RBAC:** route to **`index=salesforce`** (or `salesforce_prod` vs `salesforce_sb` macros); SOC and IAM analysts get **`srchIndexesAllowed`** without `admin`; restrict dashboard tokens exposing `Username`/`UserId` broadly.
• **Ingest economics:** default ELF poll **3600s**; pilot 7–14 days and forecast **GB/day**; trim unnecessary EventTypes if license pressure emerges; document `_indextime` vs `_time` skew monitoring for catch-up bursts.
• **Lookups & governance:** provision change-controlled CSV/KV lookups (allowlists, object classification tiers, integration registries); define **RACI** across CRM platform engineering, SOC, IAM, Data Owners, and Legal for escalations touching regulated datasets.
• **Extraction regression guard:** after each TA upgrade, run `fieldsummary` on the UC’s canonical fields for 10k recent events and archive results in Git to catch JSON path drift early.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable `salesforce:event_log` focusing on SetupAuditTrail. Key fields: Section, Action, Display, DelegateUser, Entity (if present), _time. Expand the Display regex to include internal codenames for regulated attributes maintained by your data classification team.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Schedule daily diff reviews or near-real-time alerts for production orgs:

```spl
index=salesforce sourcetype="salesforce:event_log" EventType="SetupAuditTrail" Section="Field-Level Security"
| stats values(Action) as actions, count by Display, DelegateUser, _time
| where match(Display, "(?i)(ssn|social|credit|salary|birthdate|passport|account_number)")
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL
Rows are narrowed to Field-Level Security sections, summarized by field display string and actor, then regex-filtered for high-risk vocab. `values(Action)` exposes whether visibility flipped from hidden to visible or edits targeted profiles—pair with downstream screenshots of before/after state when triaging.

Step 3 — Validate
Open each Display entry in Salesforce schema documentation, confirm whether production data exists in that field, and capture compensating controls (tokenization) if access truly broadened. Log outcomes for audit evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Feed results into GRC tools, require manager approval comments in tickets, and trend monthly drift counts toward zero for dashboarding to the chief risk officer.

Field-Level Security drift — deepening stewardship workflows
Monitoring **Display** textual changes is pragmatic but imperfect: labels can change without substantive access alteration. Complement Splunk alerting with weekly **offline reconciliation jobs** exporting Salesforce **Field Accessibility** matrices for top regulated objects comparing hash digests—not Splunk SPL focus but critical **control layering**. Document Splunk alert as **early warning**, not sole compliance proof.

Sensitive token dictionary governance
Maintain `sf_sensitive_tokens.csv` with columns `pattern`, `risk_score`, `data_owner_contact`, `review_sla_hours`. Replace inline regex progressively with **`lookup`** for audit clarity. Quarterly purge obsolete tokens superseded by tokenization architectures.

Correlation with masking projects
Coordinated uplift projects spike FLS churn—populate `masking_projects` timelines with blackout windows narrower than irresponsible full silence—use attenuated alerting (severity downgrade not suppression).

Spl pattern variants
Separate searches for **`Action` lexical categories** (“Changed field permissions”, etc.) adapting to multilingual environments by maintaining parallel token lists per locale minimally.

Operational triage emphasizing object-level narratives
Pivot from `Display` to underlying **`Entity`** using supplemental extractions (`spath`/`rex`) verified during TA upgrades — critical if `Display` truncated.

Incident closure categories
Localization noise, purposeful broadening unauthorized, purposeful authorized with ticket, tooling sync artifact, erroneous detection regex overfit.

Purple team benign broadening drill
Conduct controlled widen of faux low-risk sandbox field—not production—to validate alerting latency.

Quarterly KPI set
Median time for data owner attestation, count of fields auto-reverted after alert, stale open investigations.

Cross-link UC-10.12.48
Escalate when FLS drift coincides with toxic permission set assignment same user—composite macro `sf_composite_priv_risk`.

Summary indexing option
Materialize daily last-known state per field hash for diff visualizations.

Search performance
Pre-filter `Section="Field-Level Security"` early; avoid broad regex on entire SetupAuditTrail.

Privacy concerns
Field names may reveal sensitive context in logs—restrict dashboard access roles.

Educational outreach to citizen developers
Short guidance on using **Permission Set Groups** instead of manual FLS tweaks reducing drift noise.

Edge case: managed package field strings
Namespace noise—filter with `NOT Display="00D..."` patterns cautiously.

Edge case: formula field recalculations mis-tagged
Rare—validate sample JSON with admins.

Edge case: translation-only updates
Diff engine recommended—summary index prior label versions.

Edge case: bulk metadata API pushes
CI/CD—join deployment pipeline logs.

Edge case: scratch org ephemeral noise
Route away from prod index.

Edge case: multi-org consolidation duplicate labels
Disambiguate with org id macro token in results table.

Edge case: unicode homoglyph attacks in labels
Extremely rare—human review.

Operational metrics
Monthly count of regex pattern updates—sign of healthy governance.

Splunk ES (optional) alignment
Map to **Account Manipulation** approximations cautiously.

Documentation for PCI scoped fields
Augment tokens with `pci_flag=1` driving automatic critical severity.

Escalation to Data Protection Officer
Define threshold for DPO notification if systemic misconfiguration exposed categories.

Research horizon
Explore Salesforce **Event Monitoring** streaming for lower latency FLS awareness if licensed.

Quarterly KPI set additive
% changes with attached change documentation URL via lookup enrichment.

Training microlearning for stewards
10-minute FLS least privilege modules.

Executive summary
Show downward trend in unapproved broadening counts post training.

Correlation with DLP
If DLP triggered on field previously hidden then visible—double signal.

Incident simulation
Annual regex catastrophe exercise—ensure rollback plan for overbroad pattern.

Environmental noise from agile sprints
Sprint end spikes—calendar correlation.

Governance committee charter cross-reference
Lineage to enterprise data council minutes ID.

Bias oversight
Ensure regex sets do not accidentally target culturally specific naming conventions—review internationally.

Backup diff sampling
Monthly random sample manual comparison for assurance layer.

Customer trust
Optional external report metric on proactive FLS monitoring existence.

Third-party vendor fields
Flag vendor namespace separately for distinct thresholding.

Quarterly KPI set normalization
Drift events per thousand metadata changes.

Appendix
Versioned regex repository in Git with peer review PRs.

Cross portfolio synergy UC-10.12.47
Export spikes after FLS drift may indicate exfil post privilege widen—chain investigations.

Edge case: lightning vs classic label variance
Document mapping nuances.

Edge case: field retirement lifecycle
Renames may look like drift—integrate metadata lifecycle feed.

Edge case: encryption scheme changes
May log similarly—separate monitoring.

Edge case: profile vs permission set FLS duplication
Complex effective access—clarify narrative in runbook that Splunk indicates change event not net effective matrix always.

Edge case: experience cloud external user context
May require separate index macro.

Edge case: shield platform encryption interactions
Consult specialist—alerts still valuable for process monitoring.

Edge case: EU AI Act tangential future proofing
Document responsible logging minimization.

Quarterly KPI set recap

Psychological safety for citizen devs
Non-punitive education first strike for minor mistakes.

Metric: reopen rate
Track investigations reopened after incomplete rollback.

Search cost controls
Limit lookback macro default 30d for interactive exploration.

Historical retrain after mergers
Rebuild token lists consolidating acquired firms' naming conventions.

Governance, evidence, and continuous control testing
Establish this detection as a named IT General Control for custodial CRM data. Each quarter, export a statistically significant sample of alerts (for example, 25 cases) and perform a **quality review** covering five dimensions: (1) timeliness relative to Salesforce poll latency, (2) accuracy versus ground truth in Salesforce admin screens, (3) completeness of ticketing metadata, (4) appropriateness of suppressions, and (5) analyst narrative quality suitable for regulatory interviews. Store evidence in your GRC repository with hashes and retention aligned to your legal hold policy. When Splunk or the Salesforce TA upgrades, repeat a **regression harness** that replays frozen JSON fixtures through a dev indexer to ensure field extractions remain stable. Maintain a **change advisory** record whenever thresholds, lookup schemas, or cron schedules change, because auditors will ask for a traceable history of tuning decisions.

Performance, workload, and search optimization
Salesforce security searches can become expensive if they scan wide time ranges without partition discipline. Prefer **index-time routing** that isolates production org data, and use **`tstats` only when you have a compatible data model**—which is rarely true for raw Salesforce JSON—so focus on **fast filters** (`sourcetype`, `EventType`, `index`) and **summary indexing** for baselines. On Splunk Cloud, monitor **search budget** consumption for the service account running these schedules; split heavy baseline jobs from alert jobs. For on-premises, place collection on a **Heavy Forwarder** to keep REST polling off search heads. If you enable **workload management**, assign these searches to a pool with guardrails so ad-hoc human searches do not starve operational detections.

Risk communication and executive narrative
Translate technical hits into business language. For each incident class, maintain a one-paragraph **executive summary template** describing customer impact, financial exposure, and remediation status. This elevates the UC from a SOC tool to a board-ready control narrative, especially in financial services where CRM data includes wealth, insurance, or mortgage artifacts. Pair metrics with **leading indicators** (integration onboarding rate, active connected apps) so risk committees can ask forward-looking questions rather than only reviewing rear-view alert counts.

Deep-dive operational walkthrough — field-level security as a data stewardship partnership
Operationalizing FLS drift monitoring requires shifting culture from **“security catching admins”** to **stewardship enabling safe speed**. Begin by publishing a concise **classification guide** aligning regex tokens with organizational data taxonomy tiers. Stewards should know exactly when a flagged `Display` value implies **customer PII**, **PCI**, **privileged financial metrics**, **health data**, or merely sensitive-but-internal metrics. Misclassification breeds alert fatigue—schedule quarterly stewardship workshops to reconcile regex lists with Salesforce metadata catalogs.

Implement a dual-path review: SOC performs **triage completeness** (Was the actor authorized? Was there ticketing? Was timing suspicious relative to mergers?) while Data Owners perform **substantive correctness** (Did effective access widen in a materially harmful way?). Both must acknowledge closure in your GRC tool to satisfy dual-control narratives often requested by internal audit.

For localization-driven noise, augment regex with **`language_pack` lookups** translating canonical English tokens to equivalents rather than exploding regex infinitely—when impractical, accept localized noise attenuated via summary baselines keyed by region.

Maintain **before-and-after snapshots** selectively: excessively frequent snapshots inflate storage—but monthly snapshots for top regulated objects materially strengthen investigations. Coordinate snapshot retention schedules with Legal.

When alerting reveals **citizen developer misconfiguration**, route feedback through **CRM CoE guilds** with anonymized narratives to encourage learning—not punishment—unless malicious intent evidenced.

Purple team benign broadening drills should reinforce that alerts are **signals**, not adjudications. Train analysts explicitly on Salesforce effective access computations: FLS interplay with profiles, permission set groups, and sharing rules yields complexity Splunk alerts cannot fully encapsulate—avoid overclaiming in executive summaries.

Integrate Splunk narratives with enterprise **Master Data Management** councils when repetitive FLS churn arises from unresolved golden-record debates—underlying data problems masquerade as security telemetry noise.

Operational KPIs should emphasize **median closure time steward attestation**, not Splunk ingestion alone. Closing investigations quickly with authoritative stewardship sign-off demonstrates operating effectiveness more impressively than raw alert suppression ratios.

Historical evidence packages for regulators should juxtapose Splunk timelines with stewardship decisions, emphasizing proactive identification and corrective action—tell a **controls story**, not merely a tooling story.

Where AI-driven admin assistants emerge, anticipate **automated bulk FLS suggestions** appearing in telemetry—establish early coordination with vendors rolling out experimental features flagged in roadmap reviews.

Quarterly retrospective agenda should include stale regex debt retirement—celebrate shrinking pattern counts as metadata standardization succeeds.

Finalize by acknowledging **cultural humility**: field names evolve; stewardship partnerships must iterate continuously—a healthy sign is frequent small regex updates reflecting engagement, not firefighting silence implying neglect.

Supplemental continuity — evidence integrity, access reviews, and pattern lifecycle management
Store immutable **hashes of regex pattern files** alongside quarterly committee approvals to prove that monitoring logic changes were deliberate, peer-reviewed, and not ad-hoc edits in production. Tie FLS alert volumes to **enterprise access review cadences**: spikes preceding reviews may reflect bulk cleanup—contextualize narratives for audit committees emphasizing proactive hygiene. When migrating between Splunk Cloud stacks or changing index routing, perform **parallel run** validation for at least one full business week to guard against silent ingestion gaps that could falsely imply “no drift” when events are simply missing. Maintain a **rollback playbook** for overly broad regex deployments, including communication steps to data owners and temporary compensating monitoring via sampled administrative reports until Splunk logic is restored.

Final addendum — stewardship analytics and third-line assurance readiness
Trend **steward acknowledgment latency** by department to identify teams chronically delaying closure; partner with functional leadership to rebalance workloads rather than silently extending SLAs that undermine control intent. Maintain a **third-line sampling plan** aligning internal audit selections with statistically meaningful subsets of Splunk-notified drift events—even benign ones—to prove operating effectiveness independently of SOC self-reporting.

Continuity capsule — versioning and audit replay discipline
Maintain a changelog entry whenever Splunk SPL, lookups, Salesforce API versions, or TA builds change materially, linking to ticketing records and approval artifacts so third-line assurance can reconstruct the analytic posture for any historical timeframe without speculative interpretation.
Schedule as Alert: cron **`0 */6 * * *`** (FLS drift rarely needs sub-hour paging unless regulated), range **`-6h@h` to `now`**, trigger **>0**, throttle **`Display + DelegateUser` 24h** to avoid duplicate SetupAuditTrail fragments, severity boost when `Display` matches `pci_phi` token list even if count low.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Symptom — Regex too chatty after localization** — shift to metadata snapshot diff approach; narrow regex.  
• **Symptom — Section label drift** — Salesforce changes `Section` string; maintain `case` mapping in macro.  
• **Symptom — Steward backlog** — not Splunk failure; operational process issue—report aging KPI.  
• **Symptom — Missing `DelegateUser` for automated tool** — classify tool via `source_ip` / `SessionKey` if present.  
• **Symptom — Duplicate `_time` rows** — collapse with `stats latest(*) by Display DelegateUser date_mday`.  
• **Symptom — Cannot interpret effective access** — remind analysts Splunk shows change events, not final matrix—open Salesforce view.  
• **Symptom — Summary index diff job fails** — repair scheduled search permissions and disk quota.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the settings that decide who can read extra-sensitive columns in our customer lists, because a tiny wording change there can accidentally show social security numbers or salaries to teams that should never see them.
